mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.12% indicates deep accommodation
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 141,367 thousand reflects stable employment base
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign in post-2020 data.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline more than 2% month-over-month signals demand contraction that precedes earnings downgrades and equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 436,772 million provides baseline for decline detection
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion or early contraction phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following RSAFS declines > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.3% month-over-month while remaining positive signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and future growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 105.1786 reflects recent production levels
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth deceleration and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously by more than 1% month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand destruction across residential and consumption channels that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 1% AND RSAFS declines > 1% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 926.0 thousand and RSAFS at 436,772 million provide baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-decline instances, or joint signal has lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC difference > 0.02 in favor of single series).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains flat or declines, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes consumer retrenchment and equity selloff, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 5.4% near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession transition
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of joint-trigger instances, or interaction term adds no significant explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2%, signaling monetary policy lag that forces future rate hikes and equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 235.74 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% indicate policy accommodation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation surprise
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of policy-lag instances, or the interaction signal has lower Rank IC than FEDFUNDS alone over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle slowdown that triggers forward earnings downgrades and equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 16304.8 billion chained 2017 dollars provides baseline for growth calculation
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion or early contraction phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of low-growth quarters, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns falls below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
